issue_title: Rotation/unfold during review hides the answer (resets card to question side)

reasoning_trace.finding: Searched local source only. Found the main reviewer implementation at AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/Reviewer.kt, the superclass AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt, CardSide users, and the manifest configChanges entry.

The activity for review is com.ichi2.anki.Reviewer and it declares android:configChanges="keyboardHidden|orientation|screenSize", so rotation/screen-size changes are handled inside the running Activity instead of a normal recreate path.
Reviewer extends AbstractFlashcardViewer and initializes reviewer fields in onCreate. It starts collection loading but does not initialize a separate card-side state in Reviewer itself.
Reviewer.onSaveInstanceState only saves KEY_PREVIOUS_CARD_ID. It does not save currentCard.id or whether the answer side is currently displayed.
Reviewer.onCollectionLoaded calls updateCardAndRedraw() to start review after collection load. This is the startup path, not the answer flip path.
Reviewer.performReload() delegates to launchCatchingTask { updateCardAndRedraw() }, so any reload of the current reviewer uses the same redraw routine.

Reviewer.updateCurrentCard() pulls sched.currentQueueState(), renders the topCard, assigns currentCard = state?.topCard, and assigns queueState = state. It does not preserve or inspect the previous side.
Reviewer.displayCardQuestion() and displayCardAnswer() delegate to AbstractFlashcardViewer after reviewer-specific timer/state handling. The actual side flag changes happen in the superclass.
The Show Answer button listener calls displayCardAnswer(), so the initial answer display path is direct and not suspicious.
updateCardAndRedraw() calls updateCurrentCard(), then unconditionally calls displayCardQuestion() at line 525 before refreshing the action bar and focus.
AbstractFlashcardViewer.onConfigurationChanged() only calls super.onConfigurationChanged(newConfig) and refreshActionBar(). It does not preserve or restore isDisplayingAnswer.
NavigationDrawerActivity.onConfigurationChanged() only delegates to super and drawerToggle.onConfigurationChanged(newConfig). It does not handle card side.
The redraw/reload path fetches the current scheduler queue state and then always renders the front/question side, regardless of whether displayAnswer was true before the redraw.
displayCardQuestion() sets displayAnswer = false, renders SingleCardSide.FRONT, updates the WebView, and hides ease buttons. This directly matches the UI returning to the question side.
displayCardAnswer() sets displayAnswer = true and renders SingleCardSide.BACK. This path is correct when Show Answer is tapped.
updateCard(content) stores the rendered side HTML in cardContent and calls fillFlashcard(), so the rendered side depends on which displayCard* method was selected upstream.
fillFlashcard() loads cardContent into the WebView. It does not choose question versus answer; it only reflects the content produced earlier.

reloadWebViewContent() explicitly retains the current side by checking isDisplayingAnswer and calling displayCardAnswer() if true. This is a neighboring correct path and shows the safer behavior updateCardAndRedraw() lacks.
isDisplayingAnswer returns the companion object backing field displayAnswer, whose default is false. This global flag is the side state used by Reviewer bindings and rendering.
ViewerRefresh records queue/note/card changes only. It does not encode card side, so refreshRequired cannot restore ANSWER side after a redraw.
opExecuted() updates refreshRequired and calls refreshIfRequired(), which can trigger updateCardAndRedraw(). This refresh path also lacks side preservation.

refreshIfRequired() launches updateCardAndRedraw() when a refresh is pending and the Activity is resumed. The comment warns that updateCardAndRedraw clears refreshRequired but does not mention preserving side.

ReviewerFragment uses ReviewerViewModel.showingAnswer, but the manifest entry for the reported Activity is com.ichi2.anki.Reviewer, so this newer fragment path is not the primary legacy Activity fault.
The newer ViewModel has a showingAnswer flow and restoration logic after recreation. This reinforces that the legacy Reviewer.kt/AbstractFlashcardViewer path lacks equivalent side preservation.

reasoning_trace.target: updateCardAndRedraw() always resets to question side
reasoning_trace.finding: If a fold/rotation or related lifecycle refresh reaches updateCardAndRedraw() while the answer is visible, the unconditional displayCardQuestion() call overwrites the side state and renders SingleCardSide.FRONT.
Rendering is not the likely fault: callers explicitly pass SingleCardSide.FRONT or BACK, and displayCardAnswer() correctly passes BACK.
The WebView loading code only loads the already-rendered cardContent. It does not flip answer to question by itself.

It is relevant missing persistence, but the manifest handles orientation/screenSize without normal Activity recreation. The observed immediate question render is more directly caused by updateCardAndRedraw() choosing displayCardQuestion().

fault_candidates.reasoning: This redraw/reload method is used by Reviewer startup, reload, answer-card progression, and refresh paths. It always calls displayCardQuestion() after updating currentCard, with no check of isDisplayingAnswer/CardSide. displayCardQuestion() sets displayAnswer = false and renders SingleCardSide.FRONT, so a refresh caused during fold/rotation while the answer is visible will hide the answer and show the question side again.

final_answer.causal_chain: Reviewer is the manifest Activity for study and handles orientation/screenSize itself. The legacy reviewer stores side state in AbstractFlashcardViewer.displayAnswer/isDisplayingAnswer, but Reviewer.onSaveInstanceState does not persist CardSide and ViewerRefresh also carries no side. When a lifecycle/config-related refresh/reload reaches updateCardAndRedraw(), the method refreshes currentCard from the scheduler and then unconditionally calls displayCardQuestion(). That call sets displayAnswer = false and renders SingleCardSide.FRONT into the WebView. Therefore a card that was showing the answer before fold/rotation is redrawn as the question side, hiding the answer.
final_answer.fix_hint: Preserve the current card id and current CardSide/isDisplayingAnswer across the config-change refresh path, and have updateCardAndRedraw() restore displayCardAnswer() when the same card was already on the answer side instead of always calling displayCardQuestion().
